purpose: Test whether a fixed, label-free feature set can identify the correct class candidate already present inside a real event. This is a cross-validated feasibility probe, not a deployable method result.
claim_boundary: Development-only rankability diagnostic. It may authorize one later candidate-reranker experiment, but it is not itself evidence of held-out or OOD generalization.

cross_validation.assignment: Sort locations by sha256(fold_seed|canonical_location), then assign round-robin to folds 0 through 4.
cross_validation.fit_scope: Training folds only, including feature standardization and logistic-regression fitting.
cross_validation.pooled_primary_evaluation: Concatenate exactly one out-of-fold prediction for every eligible Val-Audit frame.

viability.failure_action: Write a blocked receipt and do not report a promotion decision.

candidate_pool.definition: Sorted union of TPA top-1 predicted classes across the frame's real sequence.
candidate_pool.query_fallback: If the candidate pool is empty, retain the STP prediction.

diagnostic_selector.standardization: Fit mean and standard deviation on candidate rows from training locations only.
diagnostic_selector.selection: Choose the candidate with maximum predicted reliability.

training_target_permutation.permutation_scope: Within each training fold; evaluation labels remain untouched.

metrics.primary: active-class macro-F1 on pooled out-of-fold predictions
secondary: fraction of the sequence-candidate oracle headroom captured

promotion_gate.pass_action: Authorize exactly one separately preregistered candidate-reranker v0 experiment.
promotion_gate.fail_action: Close candidate reliability and all further sequence-inference method development on this foundation.
promotion_gate.inconclusive_action: If bootstrap inference is invalid or a control cannot be evaluated, repair the audit without opening a held-out split and rerun the same frozen specification.
